basics.name: Migon Choi
basics.label: AI Scientist & Ph.D. in Materials Science
basics.summary: AI Scientist focused on decision-centric machine learning for scientific discovery, with dual expertise in laboratory experimentation and data-driven modeling. I specialize in uncertainty-aware modeling and data-efficient workflows for AI-driven experimental systems.

work.name: Samsung Electronics
work.summary: Designed and analyzed 8nm and 14nm RF FinFET materials architectures.
highlights: Improved device performance by mitigating body effects through electrical property and process-structure analysis.
Collaborated with cross-functional fabrication teams (photolithography, CVD, process integration) for FinFET fabrication.

work.name: Duke Capital Partners (DCP)
work.summary: Evaluated deep-tech startups in AI, materials, and infrastructure domains.
highlights: Assessed technical feasibility, scalability constraints, and uncertainty risks in early-stage technologies.
NSF National Research Traineeship (NRT) Fellow in AI for Understanding and Designing Materials (aiM)

publications.name: Chemical Information-Driven Machine Learning Models for Enhanced Performance Prediction of 2D Perovskite Solar Cells
publications.summary: Developed an interpretable ML framework to decode the relationship between chemical descriptors and device performance.

publications.name: P-type Charge Transfer Doping for Mixed Tin-Lead Halide Perovskites: Mo(tfd-COCF3)3 and F4TCNQ
publications.summary: Led experimental synthesis and characterization to understand doping effects in Sn/Pb perovskites.
